msg_reset_text: Do you want to reset all settings to default values?
msg_no_obj_text: No objects found with current threshold. Continue with original image?
help_gen_text:
To create an embroidery pattern from an open image file, click the menu item "Pattern - Generate Pattern". The following properties of the embroidery pattern can be adjusted via the "Settings - Processing Parameters" menu:

"Number of Crosses (Width)" determines how many cross stitches are placed side by side in the pattern in the horizontal direction, i.e., how wide the pattern is. The height of the pattern is determined accordingly based on the aspect ratio of the source image.

"Number of Colors (k-Means)" determines how many different yarn colors the pattern has. If the source image contains very few different shades, the actual number of colors may be lower than the value specified in the settings.

A generated pattern can be saved in JPEG or PDF format via the menu item "File - Save Pattern...". For PDF files, the page size can be specified under "Settings - Processing Parameters".
help_bg_text:
By clicking the menu item "Pattern - Pattern (No Background)", you can attempt to create an embroidery pattern that includes only the objects in the foreground of the source image. A Mask R-CNN neural network is used for background removal; this network can recognize objects from 90 categories, such as "dog" or "car." Objects that do not belong to any of these 90 categories cannot be recognized. The objects detected in the source image are treated as the foreground, and the rest of the image as the background. If no objects are detected, background removal is not performed.

You can adjust the background removal settings via the "Settings - Processing Parameters" menu as follows:

"Background Removal Model" determines which of the two available versions of the neural network is used.  If "Version 1" does not achieve satisfactory background removal, you can try "Version 2" to see if it yields better results.

"Detection Threshold" specifies the minimum probability with which the neural network must classify an object into one of the categories for the object to be considered detected. If no object is detected, you can try repeating the background removal with a lower detection threshold. The value must be greater than 0 and less than or equal to 1.

"Number of objects to keep" specifies how many objects should be assigned to the foreground. The largest objects found are selected. If fewer objects are found than the specified number, all found objects are treated as the foreground.
help_edit_text:
By clicking the menu item "Pattern - Manual Edit", you can manually edit a generated embroidery pattern. Individual cross stitches in the pattern can be deleted or assigned a different color. 

After clicking the menu item, a color palette appears showing all colors used in the pattern, along with an eraser. To recolor or delete crosses, first select the desired color or the eraser with a mouse click.  Then click on the respective crosses. You can select multiple crosses at once by holding down the right mouse button.

Changes can be undone via the menu option "Pattern - Undo Edit" or by using the keyboard shortcut Ctrl + Z.
